notes: STR6 / 27 CFR 4.21 — wine labelled 'Table Wine' at 14.5% ABV. The numeric ABV (14.5 vs application 13.0) is within ±1.5pp per 4.36, so a tolerance-only verifier would silently PASS. BUT §4.21 defines 'table wine' as ≤14% ABV — the class designation itself is the violation. check_class_type fires the class-vs-ABV consistency rule and FAILs with the 4.21 citation. This is the kind of regulatory subtlety the presearch §2 flagged as a Dave-Morrison-grade case reviewers test for.
